name: Garlic Pickles
recipeInstructions:
These are the approximate ingredients you would need to fill up a 10 liter barrel.
1. Peel garlic and cut each clove in half.
2. Peel carrots.
3. Wash vegetables.
4. Cut cabbage, carrots, and celery into quarters.
5. Break apart cauliflower. Leave cucumbers or tomatoes whole.
6. With cabbage, discard outer leaves and cut off the hard part at the base.
7. With the cauliflower, leave a little of the leaves on for taste but discard the hard part of the stem.
8. Layer vegetables so that all of one kind is not next to the other.
9. Put in the vegetables, garlic, then vegetables again, and so on.
10. Add 1 heaping tablespoon of salt for each liter of hot water that you will add to the barrel.
11. After the barrel is full with the vegetables and water, add 5 more tablespoons of salt, along with 2 tablespoons of mustard seeds, 2 tablespoons peppercorns, and 4 bay leaves. Use a cheesecloth bag or just throw them in loosely.
12. Place clean wood sticks or empty glass bottles on top to hold down all vegetables under the water if necessary. It's important for them to be covered at all times.
13. Mix the barrel (rolling it on the floor if large) when done and shake it again each day for the next few days to ensure a good mix.
14. After about 10 days, the pickles should be ready to eat. Kirby cucumbers may take a few days longer.
15. Taste the liquid after the first couple of days; you may need to add an additional tablespoon or two of salt as the salt is absorbed. Keep the balance constant and avoid adding too much salt at the beginning.
